frozen_predictions.P1: variant 1 machine-zero (L_k<1e-12) for k>kp at every z1
frozen_predictions.P2a: hard pupil -> FIELD-level D energy beyond 2kR < 1e-12 (engineered field wall)
frozen_predictions.P2b: detector-level L_k beyond 2kR (finite object window -> guard dependence)
frozen_predictions.P3: full twin nonzero tail (L_k>1e-10) on shells above 2*kp
frozen_predictions.P4: propagation never moves the ideal edge; hard field pupil -> z1-independent leak

P1.metric: max detector L_k for k>kp over all z1 (V1)

P2a_field_wall.meaning: hard Fourier pupil makes signed intensity FIELD exactly band-limited to 2kR (Thm 1 case 2) regardless of aperture

P2b_detector_wall.meaning: the finite object window re-introduces a detector-level tail beyond 2kR even with an exact field pupil; V7 naive Tukey guard reduces but does not reach 1e-12 -> an exact DETECTOR wall needs a concentration-grade (DPSS) guard (deferred to IT5). Confirms R44 binding conclusions #1/#2 and the coordinator's naive-window kill.

P3.metric: max detector L_k for k>2kp on full twin (V5)
P3.meaning: noncompact physical pedestal: frequency placement alone does NOT restore an exact wall

P4.meaning: V1 ideal support edge fixed under propagation; hard field pupil leak z1-independent (coordinator measured 3-digit invariance)

overall: KT1 confirms R44 #1. The ideal periodic code has an exact wall (P1). A hard Fourier pupil restores an EXACT FIELD-level difference-set wall to 2kR (P2a) and makes the leak z1-independent (P4), but the finite OBJECT window re-introduces a detector-level tail (P2b) that a naive window cannot remove -> an exact detector wall needs a concentration-grade guard. The unfiltered full twin has a noncompact pedestal (P3): frequency placement alone gives no wall.
